title: elatostema divaricatum
intro: Elatostema divaricatum, also known as the Indian pipevine or creeping elatostema, is a perennial plant species native to the tropical forests of the Indian subcontinent and Southeast Asia. The plant is used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which are derived from its leaves and stems. The health benefits of Elatostema divaricatum include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and promote wound healing, making it a valuable remedy in Ayurvedic and Unani medicine. Elatostema divaricatum can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and poultices, which are used to treat various ailments and promote overall well-being.
